A_buffer_margin.evidence: Bimodal distribution with ~symmetric direction balance (59.2% below-zero, 64.0% above-extent). Two distinct populations: geometry clipping (median ~40, p95 ≤ 80) and label duplication (median ~2000, max 4096). 1,695 cross-provider identity-confirmed features show identical offsets.

B_wrap_around.evidence: Direction balance is roughly symmetric across both axes and all datasets. No concentration at a specific modulus boundary. The offset distribution is continuous, not clustered at a wrap-around value.

C_decoder_bug.evidence: Step 1.1 decoder cross-validation: 0 divergences across 562 features, 13 layers, 5 dimensions. Reference: analysis/decoder-crosscheck.json

label-duplication.source: Tile compilers (Planetiler, OpenMapTiles, CARTO) intentionally duplicate label features across tile boundaries so rendering engines can display labels that span multiple tiles without clipping mid-character.
evidence: 1,695 cross-provider identity-confirmed matches via property values (name, class)
Same named features (e.g., 'Europe', 'North Atlantic Ocean') produce identical offsets across independent providers
Offsets span entire tile extent (up to 4096 = 100% of extent), consistent with labels placed at their geographic center regardless of tile boundaries
Roughly symmetric direction balance rules out directional encoding artifacts
label-duplication.specReference: MVT Specification §4.3.1 permits features to extend beyond the tile's clipping bounds for cross-tile rendering
label-duplication.note: Layer exclusion, NOT geometry-type exclusion. All 63,944 Point entries come from exactly these three layers. Point features in other layers (e.g., poi, address) retain full coordinate-range protection.

geometry-clipping-buffer.source: Tile compilers apply a clipping buffer (typically 64–80 units for extent=4096) when cutting geometry at tile boundaries. This prevents rendering gaps at tile edges where anti-aliasing or stroke width would otherwise leave visible seams.
evidence: P95 offset is exactly 80 for countries/geolines and exactly 64 for water/boundary/landcover/park/waterway
Two distinct buffer values (64 and 80) correspond to different layer configurations in tile compilers
Offsets are small relative to extent (max 80/4096 = 1.95%) — consistent with edge buffers, not placement errors
All affected layers use Polygon or LineString geometry, never Point
geometry-clipping-buffer.specReference: MVT Specification §4.3.4.3 notes that geometry may extend beyond the tile's area for buffer clipping

quantization-false-positive.source: Tile compilers quantize floating-point coordinates to integer grid. At high zoom levels with dense geometry, this can create artificial self-intersections at tile edges.
quantization-false-positive.note: Step 5 will add minimum vertex guard and optional edge tolerance
